articles.title: Israel-Iran Conflict: Iran War Live Updates: Hegseth Vows to Reopen Key Strait as U.S. Measures Fail to Calm Oil Market
articles.content: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said that the U.S. military would stop Iran from choking off the Strait of Hormuz but did not say how. Oil prices remained high even after the Trump administration eased restrictions on Russian shipments.
articles.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said that the U.S. military would stop Iran from choking off the Strait of Hormuz but did not say how. Oil prices remained high even after the Trump administration eased restrictions on Russian shipments.
articles.author: The New York Times

articles.title: How the War in Iran Could Help China and Change Asia
articles.content: American officials have said for years that they would prioritize the Indo-Pacific. Now they’re moving warships, missiles and air defenses out for a war in the Middle East.
articles.summary: American officials have said for years that they would prioritize the Indo-Pacific. Now they’re moving warships, missiles and air defenses out for a war in the Middle East.
articles.author: Damien Cave, Choe Sang-Hun, Javier C. Hernández and Eric Schmitt

articles.title: Cuba Acknowledges Talks with Trump Officials For the First Time
articles.content: President Miguel Díaz-Canel, whose country is rapidly running out of fuel, said the talks were based on “respect for the political systems of both countries.”
articles.summary: President Miguel Díaz-Canel, whose country is rapidly running out of fuel, said the talks were based on “respect for the political systems of both countries.”
articles.author: Frances Robles, David C. Adams and Patricia Mazzei

articles.title: A Visit to a Temple at the Heart of the Thailand-Cambodia Conflict
articles.content: A rare visit to a Khmer temple on Thailand and Cambodia’s border showed how deadly clashes between the two countries have scarred a heritage site.
articles.summary: A rare visit to a Khmer temple on Thailand and Cambodia’s border showed how deadly clashes between the two countries have scarred a heritage site.

articles.summary: Ukraine wants to leverage its defense expertise into security partnerships and to reap potentially vast profits for its arms industry.

articles.title: What Does It Mean to Have Air Superiority Over Iran?
articles.content: The American and Israeli air forces have a dominant advantage in the skies, but Iran can still muster some resistance.
articles.summary: The American and Israeli air forces have a dominant advantage in the skies, but Iran can still muster some resistance.

articles.title: Drone Strike Has Cyprus, and Europe, on Edge
articles.content: Allies have rushed to defend the Mediterranean nation, where the drone hit a British base. Some Cypriots wonder why the bases are still there.
articles.summary: Allies have rushed to defend the Mediterranean nation, where the drone hit a British base. Some Cypriots wonder why the bases are still there.

articles.title: Iran’s Frantic Attempt to Save Its Ships Before Torpedo Attack
articles.content: The Iranian Navy sought refuge in Sri Lanka and India. While India obliged, Sri Lanka stalled over fears it would threaten its neutrality.
articles.summary: The Iranian Navy sought refuge in Sri Lanka and India. While India obliged, Sri Lanka stalled over fears it would threaten its neutrality.

articles.title: How Russia’s Scorched-Earth Attacks Put Ukraine’s Power Grid Near Collapse
articles.content: Strikes on Ukrainian energy systems have tripled this winter. Continuous repairs and Western aid staved off a total breakdown.
articles.summary: Strikes on Ukrainian energy systems have tripled this winter. Continuous repairs and Western aid staved off a total breakdown.

articles.title: Trump Tempers María Corina Machado’s Political Ambitions in Venezuela
articles.content: President Trump is tempering the political ambitions of María Corina Machado, a Nobel laureate, as he deepens ties with her foes in Venezuela.

articles.title: China Wants Its Ethnic Minorities to Blend In. Now It’s the Law.
articles.content: Under a new “ethnic unity” law, Mandarin Chinese must now be the language of teaching. Parents must guide their children to love the Communist Party. Neighborhoods should be mixed.
articles.summary: Under a new “ethnic unity” law, Mandarin Chinese must now be the language of teaching. Parents must guide their children to love the Communist Party. Neighborhoods should be mixed.

articles.title: Ground Down by War, Hezbollah’s Loyal Base Shows Cracks
articles.content: “We just want to be back in our homes,” said a Lebanese man who, like many others in the latest round of fighting, has to flee.

articles.title: Israel Drops Case Against Soldiers Accused of Abusing Palestinian Detainee
articles.content: After a prisoner arrived at a hospital with broken ribs and a torn rectum, Israelis were once again at odds over the issue of mistreatment and impunity.
articles.summary: After a prisoner arrived at a hospital with broken ribs and a torn rectum, Israelis were once again at odds over the issue of mistreatment and impunity.

articles.title: Thousands Attend Government Rally in Tehran to Denounce Israel and U.S.
articles.content: Several senior Iranian officials showed up at the government-sponsored rally, marking Quds Day, an annual anti-Israel event that was shaken by explosions from the U.S.-Israeli aerial assault.

articles.title: How This Oil Supply Shock Compares With the Embargo of 1973
articles.content: Governments have stockpiled oil, and cars are more efficient but the supply shock is global, and there’s no sense of when it’ll end.

articles.title: Where Israeli Strikes Are Hitting Beirut
articles.content:
new video loaded: Where Israeli Strikes Are Hitting Beirut

![](https://static01.nyt.com/images/2026/03/04/world/middleeast/Thumbnail-Final-For-Real/Thumbnail-Final-For-Real-jumbo.png?auto=webp&quality=75)

2:05

Our Beirut bureau chief, Christina Goldbaum, shows how Israeli airstrikes have affected Lebanon and its capital. Hundreds of thousands of people have fled areas around Beirut and in a huge swath of southern Lebanon after Israel issued evacuation warnings in its conflict with Hezbollah.

By Christina Goldbaum, Christina Thornell, Jon Miller, Joey Sendaydiego and David Guttenfelder

March 13, 2026
articles.summary: Our Beirut bureau chief, Christina Goldbaum, shows how Israeli airstrikes have affected Lebanon and its capital. Hundreds of thousands of people have fled areas around Beirut and in a huge swath of southern Lebanon after Israel issued evacuation warnings in its conflict with Hezbollah.
articles.author: Christina Goldbaum, Christina Thornell, Jon Miller, Joey Sendaydiego and David Guttenfelder

Carney Plans Military Expansion in Canada’s Arctic, Following Trump Threats
articles.content: Prime Minister Mark Carney said Canada would build three bases in the region. The government also plans to improve infrastructure and airports in the north.

articles.content: The crash was not caused by hostile or friendly fire, U.S. Central Command said. All six crew members died, it said, bringing the number of U.S. service members killed in the Iran conflict to at least 13.
